<!-- Generated by /brand init — edit freely -->

# Jajaco Brand

## Philosophy

The grid is the design. Content is placed into a 13-column structure and the structure does all the visual work. There is no decoration, no color, no borders, no dividers. Hierarchy comes from position within the grid and from type size. Separation comes from whitespace. If a visual element doesn't place content or create structure, remove it.

## Visual Language

- **Color**: Black (#000) on white (#fff). That's the entire palette. No grays, no accent colors, no secondary text colors.
- **Grid**: 13 columns on desktop, 5 on mobile. 20px gutters. 20px page margins. This is the defining feature — every page is a composition within this grid.
- **Density**: Sparse. The grid creates generous whitespace by leaving columns empty.
- **Decoration**: None. No shadows, no gradients, no rounded corners, no background fills, no borders, no dividers.
- **Separation**: Whitespace only. No rules, no lines, no borders between sections. The grid and empty space do the work.
- **Images**: No border-radius. No border. No container. Just the image placed in the grid.

## Typography

- **Typeface**: ABC Oracle (Book), weight 400 only. No bold, no medium, no italic.
- **Size**: 12px. One size. No type scale. Hierarchy comes from grid position only.
- **Line height**: 1.4. One value.
- **Hierarchy**: Through grid position only. Never through size, weight, color, or decoration.

## Grid System

The 13-column grid is a composition canvas, not a fixed template. Each page arranges content at its own grid positions. There are no named zones — placements are bespoke per page.

- **Page grid**: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(var(--grid-columns), 1fr); gap: var(--grid-gutter);`
- **Page padding**: `--page-padding: 40px` applied on `<main>`. Header, nav, footer apply it themselves. On mobile (≤768px), `--page-padding` becomes 12px. Use `.full-bleed` to escape.
- **Full width**: `grid-column: var(--col-full)` or `grid-column: 1 / -1`
- **Placement**: use explicit `grid-column` values per element (e.g., `grid-column: 2 / 8`, `grid-column: 9 / -1`)
- **Grid utilities**: `.col-{1–13}` for start, `.span-{1–13}` for width, `.col-full` for full width, `.full-bleed` to break out of page padding.
- On mobile (≤768px), `--grid-columns` becomes 5 and `--page-padding` becomes 12px. Layouts should collapse appropriately.

Do not create named zone tokens (`--col-main`, `--col-sidebar`). The grid is a canvas — each page composes its own layout.

- **Class naming**: `.col-{N}` and `.span-{N}` are reserved for grid utilities (`grid.css`). Scoped classes for page-specific grid cells must use descriptive prefixes (e.g., `.field-title`, `.field-info`, `.appearance-date`) — never `.col-title` or `.col-date`, which collide with the utility namespace.

## Component Patterns

- **Layouts**: CSS grid with `--grid-columns`. No wrapper/card components. Content sits directly in grid cells.
- **Links**: Black text with underline. Underline disappears on hover. No color change.
- **Buttons**: Text only with underline (same as links). No border, no fill, no background. Cursor pointer.
- **Inputs**: No border, no background. Login input uses bottom border only.
- **Navigation**: `SiteNav` is the single fixed header/nav parent. It contains the logo row plus one shared row for page header and menu. `ClientNav` sits in col-13. Menu links are visible regardless of auth state. Login/logout sits under Menu when present.
- **Header**: "Ja Ja Co" split across grid — "Ja" col 1, "Ja" starts col 2 and increments by 1 on each navigation (2, 3, 4, ... 12, wraps to 2), "Co" last col. Links to home.
- **Footer**: info@jaja.co col-1, @ja_ja_co col-10, © 2026 col-12.
- **Activity indicator**: Three SVG square states — inactive (empty square), active (dotted square), client/logged-in (filled square with circular hole). Inline next to page titles.
- **Back button**: ← in col-1, uses history.back() with fallback to /.
- **Page pattern**: pages register title/status with `PageHeader`; the fixed shared nav renders back button col-1 and activity indicator + title col 2–6. Page content starts below the fixed nav.
- **Dividers**: Don't exist. Use whitespace.
- **Universal reset**: `* { font: inherit; color: inherit }` — no element gets special browser styling.

## Spacing

Spacing scale: 4 / 8 / 16 / 32 / 64 / 128 / 192 / 256. The 192px step sits between page-level and major separation. The grid gutter (20px) is a separate structural token — the spacing scale is independent. When in doubt, use more space.

**Column gaps always use `--grid-gutter`.** The geometric spacing scale applies to vertical (row) spacing only — `row-gap`, `margin-top`, `margin-bottom`, `padding-top`, `padding-bottom`. Never use spacing scale tokens for `column-gap` or horizontal `gap` on grid layouts.

Use a single spacing token. Do not combine spacing tokens with `calc()` to create intermediate values; choose the next larger or smaller token from the scale.

## Voice & Tone

- Terse. Labels are one or two words.
- Dates as `MM.DD.YY` (e.g. `03.03.26`). Relative time when recent (`3h ago`, `2 days ago`).
- No exclamation marks. No emoji. No filler.

## Page Layouts

Each page is a bespoke composition on the 13-column grid. Common patterns:

- **Standard page**: `PageHeader` registers the page title/status; fixed `SiteNav` renders BackButton col-1 and ActivityIndicator + title col 2–6. Description/content are placed by the page grid below.
- **Homepage**: desktop triptych images centered — cols 4–5 (span 2), 6–8 (span 3), 9–10 (span 2), bottom-aligned. Mobile stacks the three images vertically at col 2 / span 3. Local clock col 1, group number col 2. RecentCycles (group numbers only) below as separate grid.
- **Gated preview**: uses `PageHeader` with inactive status; notice col 2–5, stats col 7–10.
- **History detail**: mirrors homepage triptych layout + appearances grid below.
- **Index**: label col 1, links col 2–5, sections stacked vertically. Sparse.
